title: Fitting a fused spur
subtitle: Isolate the circuit, connect the new spur, and verify dead before touching anything
excerpt: How to connect a fused connection unit (fused spur) to an existing ring or radial circuit: isolating the circuit with a lock-off device, connecting the spur from a junction box or socket, and testing the new outlet.
sourceNotes: IET On-Site Guide (BS 7671, 18th Edition, publicly available guidance). Collins Complete DIY Manual. Hager and MK technical installation guides.

recipeTools.notes: For drilling the back-box hole in the wall if a surface-mounted box is not used.

glossaryTerms.definition: A fixed outlet containing a cartridge fuse (usually 3 A or 13 A) used to supply a dedicated appliance from a ring or radial circuit. Safer than a trailing lead for permanently connected appliances such as cooker hoods and bathroom shaver sockets.

A domestic power circuit where the cable leaves the consumer unit, loops around to supply sockets, and returns to the same consumer-unit terminal. The two ends form a ring. Most UK domestic sockets are on ring finals.

content.text: is connected as a branch off an existing socket or junction box on a

. Only one spur is permitted per socket on a ring circuit. In England and Wales, notifiable work under Part P of the Building Regulations includes new circuits and certain additions in bathrooms; adding a spur in other rooms from an existing socket is generally not notifiable, but check with your local authority if in doubt.
Step 1, isolate the circuit and verify dead

Switch off the MCB for the circuit at the consumer unit. Fit a lock-off device on the MCB and attach a written warning label. Use the proving unit to confirm your two-pole voltage tester is working: test the proving unit (live result). Go to the socket or junction box you will work on and test between live and neutral, live and earth, and neutral and earth: all three tests must show dead. Return to the proving unit and test again (live result). This live-dead-live sequence confirms the tester was working both before and after the dead reading.

The spur can be taken from the terminals of an existing socket (provided that socket does not already supply a spur) or from a three-terminal junction box inserted into the ring cable. Taking from a socket is simpler and does not require disturbing the cable run.

Run 2.5 mm² twin-and-earth cable from the supply point to the location of the new fused spur. The cable must be clipped at 250 mm intervals (or run in conduit or trunking) or buried in the wall to at least 50 mm depth in the 'safe zones' defined in BS 7671. Fit the back box (flush or surface) at the spur location.

At the supply socket, strip 10 mm of insulation from each conductor of the new spur cable. Sleeve the bare earth conductor in green-and-yellow sleeving. Connect brown (live) to L, blue (neutral) to N, and earth to E alongside the existing conductors. Do not remove the existing conductors; the spur is connected in parallel at the same terminals.
Step 4, connect and fit the fused spur

Connect the spur cable to the SUPPLY terminals of the fused connection unit (marked S or SUPPLY on the back of the unit). If the fused spur will connect to an appliance cable, connect the appliance lead to the LOAD terminals. Fit the correct fuse cartridge: 3 A for lighting and small appliances, 13 A for appliances up to 3 kW. Fold the cable into the back box and screw the unit faceplate home.

Remove the lock-off device and switch the MCB on. Test the original supply socket and the new fused spur for correct polarity using a socket tester. Switch on the fused spur and confirm the connected appliance operates.

attrs.symptom: MCB trips when the circuit is restored
attrs.cause: A short circuit in the new spur cable, most likely at a terminal where stripped insulation is touching an adjacent conductor.
attrs.fix: Isolate and verify dead. Open the fused spur and supply socket and check that no bare conductor is bridging terminals or touching the metal back box.

attrs.symptom: Socket tester shows reversed polarity at the fused spur
attrs.cause: Brown and blue conductors swapped at the spur supply terminal.
attrs.fix: Isolate and verify dead. Open the fused spur and swap the brown and blue conductors at the SUPPLY terminals.
